title: The One with the Ring

jokes.text: Paper cut!
jokes.setup: Character asking about diamond cuts (Tiffany cut, princess cut)
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests 'paper cut' as if it's a type of diamond cut
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey misunderstanding - he hears 'cuts' and thinks of paper cuts. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Once again, not knocking pays off.
jokes.setup: Joey walked in on someone looking at ring brochures
jokes.punchline: Joey celebrates his habit of not knocking as beneficial
jokes.explanation: Very Joey - turning his bad habit into a life philosophy. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I wish you hadn't been on the toilet.
jokes.setup: Context of Joey walking in without knocking
jokes.punchline: Revealing the awkward bathroom scenario
jokes.explanation: Standard bathroom humor setup, gets the job done but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Is there such a thing as a Monica cut? Because that would be you best bet.
jokes.setup: Discussion about diamond cuts for Monica's engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests a cut named after Monica specifically
jokes.explanation: Sweet Joey logic - if there are cuts named after things, why not Monica? Shows his caring in a funny way.

jokes.text: How rude. Sorry. Want a bite?
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler blow off Monica and Rachel
jokes.punchline: Ross calls them rude then immediately does something rude
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic moment showing Ross's obliviousness to his own rudeness.

jokes.text: You just have to think of him as a jar of pickles that won't open.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking about getting Paul to open up emotionally
jokes.punchline: Phoebe compares emotional unavailability to a stubborn jar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe metaphor - completely unexpected but somehow makes sense. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I should run him under hot water and bang his head against a table?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's pickle jar metaphor
jokes.punchline: Rachel takes the metaphor literally to violent extremes
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Phoebe's metaphor. The violent imagery is unexpected and funny.

jokes.text: That's what you do when you want to get the truth out of someone.
jokes.setup: Rachel's violent interpretation of the pickle metaphor
jokes.punchline: Phoebe matter-of-factly confirms this as interrogation technique
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's deadpan delivery makes the absurd violence seem reasonable. Classic Phoebe logic.

jokes.text: Can't complain.
jokes.setup: Phoebe demonstrating how to get Paul to open up
jokes.punchline: Paul gives the most generic, closed response possible
jokes.explanation: Perfectly demonstrates Paul's emotional unavailability. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Paul's awkward quick exit reaction
jokes.setup: Paul realizing Ross is there and the engagement ring situation
jokes.punchline: Paul's visible discomfort and hasty departure
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward exit moment. Gets a reaction but nothing special.

jokes.text: Come on. You're a paleontologist. Dig a little deeper.
jokes.setup: Ross can't think why Chandler would be mad
jokes.punchline: Monica uses Ross's profession in a dig/excavation pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that incorporates Ross's specific job. Well-constructed pun with good delivery setup.

jokes.text: You should run him under hot water and bang his head against the table.
jokes.setup: Ross talking about wanting Chandler to tell him the truth
jokes.punchline: Phoebe repeats her violent interrogation advice
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Phoebe's consistency. Not as funny as original but effective repetition.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's great, Paul. But, you know... I want to know what.. Wow, those are really great!
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to get Paul to open up emotionally
jokes.punchline: Rachel gets distracted by Paul's physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Standard physical attraction humor. Works for the character but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: They say that still waters run deep, and I want to swim in yours.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to get Paul to open up with poetic language
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic metaphor that sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Cringey romantic metaphor that's trying too hard. The awkwardness is the joke.

jokes.text: Are you talking about having sex?
jokes.setup: Rachel's overly poetic 'swimming in still waters' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Paul's blunt, literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation. Paul's literal-mindedness punctures Rachel's romantic pretension efficiently.

jokes.text: Your friends have a slumber party and stick your hand in warm water... while you sleep, so you pee in your sleeping bag.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking Paul about painful puberty experiences
jokes.punchline: Rachel overshares her own humiliating experience
jokes.explanation: Classic overshare moment. Rachel's attempt to relate leads to TMI about her own embarrassment.

jokes.text: You're lucky you never met that bitch, Sharon Majeski.
jokes.setup: Rachel's slumber party bedwetting story
jokes.punchline: Rachel's lingering resentment toward specific childhood tormentor
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Rachel still holds grudges from childhood. The specific name makes it funnier.

jokes.text: When I was 6 years old, I wanted a Big Wheel. And instead, my parents got me this little... plastic chicken that you hop around on.
jokes.setup: Paul finally opening up about his childhood
jokes.punchline: Pathetic substitute for the toy he actually wanted
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - shows Paul's disappointment in a specific, relatable way. The plastic chicken is a funny detail.

jokes.text: They called me 'Chicken Boy.'
jokes.setup: Paul sharing his childhood trauma about the plastic chicken toy
jokes.punchline: The humiliating nickname that resulted
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. The nickname is both pathetic and memorable, showing why Paul is so closed off.

jokes.text: Chicken?
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning Kung Pao chicken at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Paul's traumatic reaction to the word 'chicken'
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that's perfectly timed and character-integrated. One word delivers maximum comedic impact.

jokes.text: Chicken Boy! I'm sorry. I didn't mean to do that.
jokes.setup: Paul's traumatic reaction to hearing 'chicken'
jokes.punchline: Rachel accidentally calling him by his childhood nickname
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the chicken callback. Rachel's immediate regret adds to the cringe factor.

jokes.text: Ugly ring, ugly ring, ugly ring...
jokes.setup: Chandler looking at ring after ring trying to find the perfect one
jokes.punchline: His increasingly frustrated dismissal of every option
jokes.explanation: Shows Chandler's perfectionist anxiety about the proposal. Basic escalation but fits character.

jokes.text: It's a beautiful selection.
jokes.setup: Chandler just called all the rings ugly
jokes.punchline: Employee's polite but strained response
jokes.explanation: Basic service industry politeness humor. Gets a small reaction but nothing memorable.

jokes.text: An engagement bracelet or an engagement tiara! An engagement Revolutionary War musket!
jokes.setup: Suggesting alternatives to an engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd suggestions culminating in a weapon
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe absurdism. The escalation from jewelry to antique weapon is perfectly unexpected and character-specific.

jokes.text: Imagine getting down on one knee... and handing her this gorgeous piece of weaponry?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's Revolutionary War musket suggestion
jokes.punchline: Phoebe genuinely picturing a romantic weapon proposal
jokes.explanation: Great follow-up that doubles down on the absurdity. 'Gorgeous piece of weaponry' is a perfect oxymoron.

jokes.text: This is why you brought me. I know how to haggle.
jokes.setup: Phoebe confidently taking charge of ring negotiation
jokes.punchline: Sets up expectation that she's good at negotiating
jokes.explanation: Sets up Phoebe's terrible haggling skills. Good setup but payoff is what makes it work.

jokes.text: I would like to see that ring. Or not. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming to be a good haggler
jokes.punchline: Immediately showing terrible negotiation by being completely passive
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expectation. Phoebe's 'haggling' is the opposite of aggressive negotiation.

jokes.text: Sir, could I ask you...? Could you hold out that ring and ask me to marry you?
jokes.setup: Chandler looking at engagement rings
jokes.punchline: Asking male jewelry store employee to simulate proposal
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Chandler's romantic anxiety. The awkwardness of the request is funny.

jokes.text: We will give you $10.
jokes.setup: $8600 ring, Chandler offers $8000, seller accepts $8000
jokes.punchline: Phoebe 'negotiates' down to $10
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to her haggling setup. The absurdly low offer after a deal was already struck is peak Phoebe.

jokes.text: We stand firm at $10.
jokes.setup: Seller trying to process the $8000 payment
jokes.punchline: Phoebe doubling down on her ridiculous $10 offer
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the terrible haggling. 'Stand firm' makes it sound like serious business negotiation.

jokes.text: Yes. I have these two rather beautiful... five-dollar bills.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking what's available for $10
jokes.punchline: Employee offering literal money as merchandise
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to Phoebe's absurd request. The 'beautiful five-dollar bills' description is funny.

jokes.text: I'll give you $1 for them.
jokes.setup: Employee offering two five-dollar bills for $10
jokes.punchline: Phoebe trying to negotiate down below face value
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Phoebe doesn't understand basic economics. Offering $1 for $10 is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: We were supposed to just get him one? That guy's always mooching off of us.
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting they should have gotten Chandler a Knicks ticket
jokes.punchline: Joey calling Chandler a mooch while using his credit card
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment. Joey's obliviousness to his own mooching while criticizing Chandler is very in character.

jokes.text: Sometimes I just don't get Chandler. We do stuff all the time without you, and you don't get all upset.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to understand why Chandler is upset about being excluded
jokes.punchline: Joey accidentally revealing they exclude Ross regularly
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey foot-in-mouth moment. His attempt to defend himself makes it worse.

jokes.text: All the time? All the time.
jokes.setup: Joey's revelation that they exclude Ross frequently
jokes.punchline: Ross's hurt realization and Joey's confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Joey's accidental revelation. The repetition emphasizes Ross's hurt feelings.

jokes.text: Still crying? Like a little girl.
jokes.setup: Paul's emotional breakdown continues
jokes.punchline: Confirmation that the opening up backfired spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Shows the consequence of Rachel's success in getting Paul to open up. The 'little girl' comparison is harsh but funny.

jokes.text: I didn't know that I was gonna unleash... this weepy, clingy, moist monster!
jokes.setup: Rachel regretting getting Paul to open up emotionally
jokes.punchline: 'Moist monster' description of emotional Paul
jokes.explanation: Great escalating description. 'Moist monster' is particularly vivid and disgusting in a funny way.

jokes.text: I only know of two ways to shut a man up. One of them is sex.
jokes.setup: Rachel needs to stop Paul from talking
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's practical but inappropriate solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe bluntness. Sets up expectation for the second method which makes the follow-up funnier.

jokes.text: What's the other? Don't know. Never had to use it.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking about the second way to shut a man up
jokes.punchline: Phoebe reveals sex always works so she's never needed plan B
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline structure and very Phoebe. The implication about her sex life is both confident and funny.

jokes.text: That's right. You're the talker.
jokes.setup: Discussion about sex stopping men from talking
jokes.punchline: Phoebe implying Rachel talks during sex
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Phoebe's knowledge of her friends' habits. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: We have some here. No, you don't!
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she needs to buy Paul more tissues
jokes.punchline: Rachel's desperate denial when Phoebe offers tissues they have
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel's desperation to escape the Paul situation. Her vehement denial of obvious facts is funny.

jokes.text: What do you think? Too much? A tad.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying on the tiara Phoebe suggested
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's understated reaction to obviously ridiculous sight
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback. 'A tad' is perfect understatement for a man wearing a tiara.

jokes.text: Take the tiara back... and let me hold the musket again.
jokes.setup: Chandler deciding the tiara is too much
jokes.punchline: Requesting the even more ridiculous musket option
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - if a tiara is too much, logically a musket must be just right. Absurd Chandler logic.

jokes.text: Let me out! Now!
jokes.setup: Chandler seeing the ring he wanted being sold to someone else
jokes.punchline: Chandler trapped in jewelry store security doors
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation of Chandler's panic. Standard slapstick but works for the situation.

jokes.text: Chandler... did your dad ever hug you? No. Did he hug you?
jokes.setup: Paul in emotional overshare mode approaching Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan deflection of emotional question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - deflects emotional vulnerability with humor. Very character-specific defense mechanism.

jokes.text: You can see my dad in Vegas kissing other dads.
jokes.setup: Paul sharing about missing his dad
jokes.punchline: Chandler's casual reference to his dad's unusual Vegas lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler line - dark humor about his family issues delivered casually. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Would you hug me? I'm a little busy here, Paul. That's what my dad used to say.
jokes.setup: Paul asking Chandler for a hug
jokes.punchline: Paul's hurt reaction connecting rejection to childhood trauma
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation. Paul's ability to connect everything to childhood trauma is both pathetic and funny.

jokes.text: Okay. A quick one. Come on, hug it out.
jokes.setup: Paul's emotional manipulation about his dad
jokes.punchline: Chandler reluctantly agreeing to awkward hug
jokes.explanation: Chandler's uncomfortable attempt to be kind while setting boundaries. 'Hug it out' tries to make it casual.

jokes.text: Five more seconds.. Okay!
jokes.setup: The 'quick' hug that Paul won't let end
jokes.punchline: Paul clinging longer than appropriate, Chandler's growing discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the awkward hug. Shows how Paul's emotional neediness makes everyone uncomfortable.

jokes.text: My back pocket! Thank God.
jokes.setup: Chandler desperately needing his credit card from Joey
jokes.punchline: Awkward physical comedy of retrieving wallet from tight pocket
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy. The relief in 'Thank God' suggests it was awkward to retrieve.

jokes.text: They put you in jail? The little jail between the doors!
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if Phoebe was really put in jail
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic description of jewelry store security vestibule
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe escalation. 'Little jail between the doors' is both accurate and dramatically overblown.

jokes.text: Yeah, but when he proposed to me with the ring, I got goosebumps.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying Monica never saw the other ring so any ring will work
jokes.punchline: Chandler admitting emotional reaction to jewelry store employee's fake proposal
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Chandler's romantic side and anxiety about getting proposal perfect.

jokes.text: Maybe it was the guy. It was the ring!
jokes.setup: Chandler getting goosebumps from fake proposal
jokes.punchline: Phoebe suggesting romantic feelings for employee, Chandler's vehement denial
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the awkward proposal moment. Chandler's insistence makes it funnier.

jokes.text: We'll be the new Joey and Chandler. What?
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey planning to freeze out Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ross casually suggesting they're better friends than Joey and Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing Ross's competitiveness even with friendships. Joey's confused reaction sells it.

jokes.text: Hey, don't get angry. We turn on each other, he wins.
jokes.setup: Joey getting defensive about his friendship with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ross treating friend drama like military strategy
jokes.explanation: Good Ross moment - overthinking friend dynamics with strategic thinking. Sounds like a war general.

jokes.text: It gets me kind of hot. Wait, wait. Listen to this:
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to use sex to stop Paul from talking
jokes.punchline: Paul completely missing sexual cues to share more stories
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Phoebe's advice that completely fails. Paul's obliviousness makes Rachel's plan backfire.

jokes.text: Whatever happened to that little dude? So full of dreams.
jokes.setup: Paul sharing about wanting to be a surfer
jokes.punchline: Paul getting overly philosophical about his childhood dreams
jokes.explanation: Shows Paul's descent into self-pitying nostalgia. The overly dramatic tone is cringey in a funny way.

jokes.text: I don't care about the little dude!
jokes.setup: Paul's continued emotional oversharing about his childhood
jokes.punchline: Rachel's frustrated outburst breaking character
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Rachel's frustration. 'Little dude' repetition makes it funnier than just saying 'you.'

jokes.text: That's only because they get $100 an hour! Do you know how much money I could have made listening to you? $2000!
jokes.setup: Rachel's rant about Paul needing professional help
jokes.punchline: Rachel calculating her lost earnings from listening to Paul
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Rachel's frustration through financial calculation. The specific $2000 makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Know when I figured that out? While you were talking!
jokes.setup: Rachel's calculation of lost therapy earnings
jokes.punchline: Rachel revealing she did math during Paul's emotional sharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how completely Rachel checked out. The timing revelation makes it extra brutal.

jokes.text: It's just that when those gates open... it's hard to close them.
jokes.setup: Paul explaining his emotional outburst
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic metaphor about emotional vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Paul continues his overly dramatic emotional metaphors. The 'gates' imagery is pretentious and cringey.

jokes.text: I can't believe you talked me into this gumball-machine-looking ring.
jokes.setup: Chandler stuck with the backup ring
jokes.punchline: Chandler's unflattering description of the ring's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good visual description that helps audience imagine the ring. 'Gumball-machine' is vivid and insulting.

jokes.text: Unless I look at it really closely, and then I can see my own eye.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining how looking at current ring makes him feel nothing
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal description of ring reflection
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler observation - taking the romantic ring-gazing concept to absurd literal conclusion.

jokes.text: Somewhere Over the...? L'Espace. Rainbow L'Espace! No, just L'Espace.
jokes.setup: Trying to remember restaurant name starting with rainbow
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's creative but wrong connection to 'Over the Rainbow'
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe logic - making connections that don't exist. The 'Rainbow L'Espace' combination is absurd.

jokes.text: I'd love to know how you got from rainbow to L'Espace. No, you wouldn't. You don't wanna get in here.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about Phoebe's rainbow logic
jokes.punchline: Phoebe warning him away from her thought process
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe meta-humor. She's self-aware that her logic is incomprehensible and protects others from it.

jokes.text: Wow, you're good! After this, we should solve crimes.
jokes.setup: Phoebe correctly identifying the proposal situation from distance
jokes.punchline: Chandler's suggestion they become detective partners
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Phoebe's observational skills. The crime-solving suggestion is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Wow, I would trade. It is beautiful, but I'm gonna use this one. Excuse me.
jokes.setup: Chandler offering to trade rings
jokes.punchline: Man acknowledging better ring but keeping original anyway
jokes.explanation: Shows how reasonable people behave vs. Chandler's panic. The casual dismissal adds to Chandler's frustration.

jokes.text: Do you want to break her heart? Yeah. Do you want to break a dying woman's heart?
jokes.setup: Chandler's emotional appeal about his fiancée
jokes.punchline: Phoebe escalating to fake terminal illness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of manipulation tactics. Phoebe's immediate leap to death is perfectly over-the-top.

jokes.text: Yeah, she's dying... of a cough, apparently.
jokes.setup: Customer asking if Phoebe is really dying
jokes.punchline: Chandler's reluctant confirmation with weak diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler delivery - reluctantly playing along while subtly undermining the lie. 'Apparently' sells his discomfort.

jokes.text: It's my dying wish to have that ring. If I'm not buried with it... my spirit will wander the netherworld..
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming to be dying
jokes.punchline: Elaborate supernatural consequences for not getting ring
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe absurdism. The netherworld escalation is perfectly over-the-top and supernatural.

jokes.text: That's enough! Oh, I don't know.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's elaborate dying wish speech
jokes.punchline: Chandler cutting her off then second-guessing himself
jokes.explanation: Good reaction showing Chandler's embarrassment but also desperation. The second-guessing is funny.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm marrying a dead woman!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's fake dying woman story
jokes.punchline: Customer's realization of the bizarre situation he's in
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the fake dying story. His confused excitement about his morbid situation is funny.

jokes.text: I think we gotta end the freeze-out.
jokes.setup: Chandler announcing his engagement plans
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately abandoning their freeze-out strategy
jokes.explanation: Shows how quickly the guys abandon petty fights for big news. Ross's strategic retreat is in character.

jokes.text: We're gonna be brothers-in-law! Come here!
jokes.setup: Ross realizing Chandler will marry Monica
jokes.punchline: Ross's sudden emotional reaction to family connection
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's tendency to get emotional about family connections. The sudden hug request is sweet but funny.

jokes.text: I think I'm gonna cry. Oh! No more crying! I just dumped one crybaby. I'll dump you too.
jokes.setup: Joey getting emotional about Chandler's engagement
jokes.punchline: Rachel's threat based on her Paul experience
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Rachel's Paul trauma. Her immediate shutdown of male crying is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Oh, nice. One and a half carat, easy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe looking at Chandler's engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's expert appraisal of diamond quality
jokes.explanation: Shows Phoebe's unexpected expertise in jewelry appraisal. The casual 'easy' suggests she knows her diamonds.

jokes.text: You told her before you told us? She walked in while I was looking at brochures. You can understand that.
jokes.setup: Ross upset Phoebe knew about engagement first
jokes.punchline: Chandler referencing Phoebe's earlier discovery
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the opening scene. Shows how secrets are hard to keep in this friend group.

jokes.text: You should know. You've bought like a billion of them.
jokes.setup: Ross commenting on how beautiful Chandler's ring choice is
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's brutal reminder of Ross's multiple divorces
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe brutal honesty. The exaggeration to 'billion' makes Ross's divorce history funny rather than sad.

jokes.text: Humor based on my pain.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's joke about Ross's multiple marriages
jokes.punchline: Ross's meta-commentary on being the group's punching bag
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment where Ross acknowledges his role as the group's target. Self-aware and quotable.

jokes.text: Of all of your wives, I'm the only one who never got a ring.
jokes.setup: Discussion about engagement rings
jokes.punchline: Reference to Ross's ex-wife who left him for a woman
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ross's first marriage ending. The ring complaint adds insult to injury about his romantic failures.

jokes.text: Something other than the divorce papers and the hangover.
jokes.setup: Wanting a memento from marriage to Ross
jokes.punchline: The only mementos being negative consequences
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how completely Ross's first marriage failed. 'Hangover' suggests even the wedding was regrettable.

jokes.text: But ask for a musket. Ask for a musket!
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ross not giving wedding rings
jokes.punchline: Phoebe still pushing her Revolutionary War musket idea
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Phoebe's musket obsession. Her persistence with the absurd idea is very Phoebe.

jokes.text: Well, yeah. No ring, no sex. Big surprise. Get me a musket and we'll talk.
jokes.setup: Ross mentioning never getting sex in his first marriage
jokes.punchline: Carol connecting lack of romance to sexual problems, then musket callback
jokes.explanation: Great ending callback tying together Ross's romantic failures with Phoebe's musket obsession. Dark but funny.

tmdb_overview: Chandler and Phoebe search for Monica's ring; Phoebe loses it. Joey and Ross misunderstand, giving cold shoulder. Rachel dates Paul.
